rawText:
The war in Iran has handed Russia an unexpected financial reprieve, but the underlying pressures on its military and economy continue to mount. With oil prices surging back to around a hundred dollars a barrel after months of decline, and Western sanctions weakened by the energy crisis, the Kremlin is enjoying a windfall that could partially offset years of economic damage. Yet on the battlefield in Ukraine, Russia faces a new kind of attrition it may not be able to sustain.

Before the Gulf conflict, Russia's energy revenues had been collapsing. Indian and Chinese refiners had sharply reduced purchases ahead of new sanctions, and export volumes fell by a fifth. The budget deficit in just the first two months of the year reached nine-tenths of the target for all of 2026. The closure of the Strait of Hormuz reversed this trajectory almost overnight, as global buyers scrambled for alternative crude. Russian oil, similar in quality to Gulf varieties, is now selling at a premium rather than a steep discount. India has increased purchases by roughly half, and the American administration has issued waivers allowing broader buying, undermining the very sanctions regime it had previously tightened.

Yet high oil prices alone cannot solve Russia's deeper problems. Ukrainian drone forces have achieved what analysts consider a turning point in the war: since December, verified Russian losses to drones have exceeded recruitment for the first time. A gamified targeting system that prioritizes individual infantry over armored vehicles has driven enemy casualties to nearly four hundred per day at peak periods, the equivalent of an entire battalion's assault component. The exchange rate is starkly asymmetric, with four hundred Russian casualties for every Ukrainian one, at a material cost of under nine hundred dollars per kill. Russia continues to gain little ground despite these losses.

The geopolitical dimension adds further complexity. Ukraine and its European allies are experiencing their first significant friction, driven by disputes over Russian oil still flowing through pipelines, disagreements over the pace of EU accession, and frustration at American disengagement. Hungary has blocked a ninety-billion-euro support package over the pipeline dispute. Despite these tensions, the fundamental alignment between Ukraine and Europe remains intact, and both sides recognize they cannot afford a genuine rift.

Russia's windfall may buy time but not a path to victory. Every ten-dollar increase in oil prices delivers roughly 1.6 billion dollars to the Kremlin monthly, yet the military cannot convert money into territorial gains. Oil production is declining at roughly three percent annually as investment is diverted to repairs, and the civilian economy has been hollowed out by mobilization and sanctions. The sugar high from the Gulf crisis masks a slow deterioration that no amount of elevated crude prices can reverse.

sentences.original: The war in Iran has handed Russia an unexpected financial reprieve, but the underlying pressures on its military and economy continue to mount.
sentences.translation: 이란 전쟁은 러시아에 뜻밖의 재정적 유예를 안겨주었지만, 군사력과 경제에 대한 근본적인 압박은 계속 가중되고 있다.

grammarElements.text: but the underlying pressures ... continue to mount
grammarElements.label: but 대조절

vocabulary.example: The new funding offered a brief reprieve from the company's cash crisis.
Pressure continued to mount on the government to act decisively.

expressions.usage: 압박, 비용, 긴장 등이 점차 심해질 때 사용하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Event] has handed [recipient] [benefit], but [contrasting reality]
patterns.explanation: 어떤 사건이 이익을 가져다주었지만 근본적 문제는 해결하지 못함을 대조하는 구조
examples: The tax cut has handed consumers extra spending power, but inflation continues to erode real wages.
The ceasefire has handed the region temporary peace, but underlying ethnic tensions remain unresolved.

drillQuestions.question: The war in Iran has ______ Russia an unexpected financial reprieve.

sentences.notes: 기사의 핵심 대조를 설정하는 도입문. hand someone something 수여동사 구문과 but 이하의 대조 구조가 전체 논지를 압축한다.
pronunciationNotes: reprieve [rɪˈpriːv] -- '리프리이브'로 두 번째 음절에 강세, 장모음

sentences.original: With oil prices surging back to around a hundred dollars a barrel after months of decline, and Western sanctions weakened by the energy crisis, the Kremlin is enjoying a windfall that could partially offset years of economic damage.
sentences.translation: 유가가 수개월간의 하락 이후 배럴당 약 100달러로 다시 급등하고 서방 제재가 에너지 위기로 약화된 상황에서, 크렘린은 수년간의 경제적 피해를 부분적으로 상쇄할 수 있는 뜻밖의 횡재를 누리고 있다.

grammarElements.text: With oil prices surging back ... and Western sanctions weakened ...
grammarElements.label: with + 분사구문 (병렬)

grammarElements.text: a windfall that could partially offset years of economic damage
grammarElements.label: 관계절을 포함한 목적어

vocabulary.example: The company received a windfall from the sudden spike in commodity prices.
Higher exports helped offset the decline in domestic demand.

expressions.usage: 가격이나 수치가 한때 하락했다가 다시 빠르게 상승할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: With [condition A] and [condition B], [subject] is [verb-ing] [result]
patterns.explanation: 두 가지 배경 조건을 with 분사구문으로 제시한 뒤 주절에서 결과를 서술하는 구조
examples: With demand recovering and supply constrained, manufacturers are enjoying their strongest quarter in years.
With interest rates falling and confidence rising, investors are pouring money into equities.

drillQuestions.question: The Kremlin is enjoying a ______ that could partially offset years of economic damage.

sentences.notes: With + 분사구문이 두 개 병렬로 연결되어 배경 상황을 압축적으로 전달한다. surging(능동)과 weakened(수동)의 대비에 주목.

koreanAlignment.en: partially offset years of economic damage

sentences.original: Yet on the battlefield in Ukraine, Russia faces a new kind of attrition it may not be able to sustain.
sentences.translation: 그러나 우크라이나 전장에서 러시아는 감당하지 못할 수도 있는 새로운 유형의 소모전에 직면해 있다.

grammarElements.text: a new kind of attrition it may not be able to sustain
grammarElements.label: 관계절을 포함한 목적어 (접촉절)

vocabulary.example: The company lost market share through slow attrition rather than a single failure.

expressions.usage: 기존과 질적으로 다른 현상을 소개할 때 사용하는 표현

patterns.pattern: Yet [subject] faces [challenge] it may not be able to [verb]
patterns.explanation: 앞선 긍정적 내용에 역접으로 전환하여 감당하기 어려운 도전을 제시하는 구조
examples: Yet the industry faces a labor shortage it may not be able to overcome.
Yet the government faces a credibility gap it may not be able to bridge.

drillQuestions.question: Russia faces a new kind of ______ it may not be able to sustain.

sentences.notes: 첫 문단의 마무리 문장으로, Yet이 앞선 '횡재' 논의에서 '전장의 현실'로 전환하는 역할을 한다. 관계대명사가 생략된 접촉절 구조에 주목.

koreanAlignment.en: it may not be able to sustain

sentences.original: Before the Gulf conflict, Russia's energy revenues had been collapsing.
sentences.translation: 걸프 분쟁 이전에 러시아의 에너지 수입은 급감하고 있었다.

vocabulary.example: Consumer spending was collapsing as unemployment soared.

expressions.usage: 석유, 가스 등 에너지 자원 판매로 얻는 국가 수입을 가리키는 경제 용어

patterns.pattern: Before [event], [subject] had been [verb-ing]
patterns.explanation: 특정 사건 이전에 이미 진행 중이었던 상황을 과거완료 진행형으로 표현하는 시제 패턴
examples: Before the merger, the company had been struggling with debt.
Before the election, support for the party had been declining steadily.

drillQuestions.question: Before the Gulf conflict, Russia's energy revenues ______ collapsing.

sentences.notes: 과거완료 진행형(had been collapsing)은 걸프 분쟁이라는 특정 시점 이전부터 이미 진행 중이었던 하락세를 강조한다.

sentences.original: Indian and Chinese refiners had sharply reduced purchases ahead of new sanctions, and export volumes fell by a fifth.
sentences.translation: 인도와 중국의 정유업체들이 새로운 제재를 앞두고 구매를 급격히 줄였고, 수출량은 5분의 1 감소했다.

grammarElements.text: and export volumes fell by a fifth
grammarElements.label: 등위절

vocabulary.example: Asian refiners turned to alternative suppliers when Gulf exports were disrupted.
Output dropped by a fifth within just three months.

expressions.usage: 시간적으로 어떤 사건이 발생하기 전에 미리 행동했음을 나타내는 전치사구

patterns.pattern: [Subject] had [adverb] [past participle] [object] ahead of [event]
patterns.explanation: 어떤 사건을 예상하고 미리 행동을 취했음을 과거완료로 표현하는 구조
examples: Investors had quietly sold shares ahead of the earnings report.
Governments had already stockpiled supplies ahead of the expected shortage.

drillQuestions.question: Export volumes fell by a ______.

sentences.notes: by a fifth와 같은 분수 표현은 영어 경제 기사에서 매우 빈번하게 등장한다. by a third(3분의 1), by half(절반) 등도 함께 익혀두자.

sentences.original: The budget deficit in just the first two months of the year reached nine-tenths of the target for all of 2026.
sentences.translation: 올해 처음 두 달 만에 재정 적자가 2026년 전체 목표의 10분의 9에 달했다.

grammarElements.text: in just the first two months of the year
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사구 (강조)

grammarElements.text: nine-tenths of the target for all of 2026
grammarElements.label: 목적어

vocabulary.example: The budget deficit widened to its highest level in a decade.

expressions.usage: 숫자를 분수로 표현하여 심각성을 강조하는 기법. 90%라고 쓰는 것보다 저널리즘적 무게감이 있다

patterns.pattern: [Metric] in just [short time period] reached [fraction] of [longer-period target]
patterns.explanation: 짧은 기간에 장기 목표의 대부분을 이미 소진했음을 강조하는 시간 대비 구조
examples: Emissions in just the first quarter reached two-thirds of the annual cap.
Spending in just six months reached four-fifths of the yearly allocation.

drillQuestions.question: The budget deficit reached ______ of the target for all of 2026.

sentences.notes: just가 시간 부사구에 삽입되어 '겨우 두 달 만에'라는 충격적 속도를 강조한다. 분수 표현(nine-tenths)은 영어 학술/저널리즘 문체의 핵심 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: in just the first two months

sentences.original: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz reversed this trajectory almost overnight, as global buyers scrambled for alternative crude.
sentences.translation: 호르무즈 해협의 봉쇄가 이 궤적을 거의 하룻밤 사이에 뒤집었고, 전 세계 구매자들이 대안 원유를 확보하려고 안간힘을 썼다.

grammarElements.text: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: as global buyers scrambled for alternative crude
grammarElements.label: as 이유/동시절

vocabulary.example: The economy's trajectory shifted dramatically after the policy change.
Airlines scrambled to rebook passengers after the cancellations.

expressions.usage: 변화가 극적으로 빠르게 일어났음을 강조하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Event] reversed [trend] almost overnight, as [actors] scrambled for [resource]
patterns.explanation: 하나의 사건이 기존 추세를 급격히 뒤집고 시장 참여자들이 대안을 급히 찾는 상황을 묘사하는 패턴
examples: The trade ban reversed the surplus almost overnight, as importers scrambled for alternative sources.
The policy shift reversed the hiring freeze almost overnight, as firms scrambled for talent.

drillQuestions.question: Global buyers ______ for alternative crude.

sentences.notes: reversed this trajectory라는 표현은 경제/정치 기사에서 추세 전환을 설명할 때 핵심적으로 사용된다.

koreanAlignment.en: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz

sentences.original: Russian oil, similar in quality to Gulf varieties, is now selling at a premium rather than a steep discount.
sentences.translation: 걸프산 원유와 품질이 유사한 러시아 원유는 이제 급격한 할인이 아닌 프리미엄을 받고 판매되고 있다.

grammarElements.text: similar in quality to Gulf varieties
grammarElements.label: 형용사구 삽입 (동격)

grammarElements.text: at a premium rather than a steep discount
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (대조)

vocabulary.example: Organic produce sells at a premium in most supermarkets.
The steep decline in share prices alarmed investors.

expressions.phrase: at a premium rather than a steep discount
expressions.usage: 이전 상황과의 극적 반전을 대조하여 보여주는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject], [appositive modifier], is now [verb-ing] at [A] rather than [B]
patterns.explanation: 삽입 수식어로 주어를 보충 설명한 뒤, A rather than B 구조로 이전 상황과 현재를 대조하는 패턴
examples: The currency, once considered unstable, is now trading at a premium rather than a discount.
The region, long overlooked by investors, is now attracting capital rather than losing it.

drillQuestions.question: Russian oil is now selling at a ______ rather than a steep discount.

sentences.notes: similar in quality to Gulf varieties라는 삽입구가 왜 러시아 원유가 대안으로 각광받는지를 설명해주는 핵심 정보이다.

koreanAlignment.en: similar in quality to Gulf varieties

sentences.original: India has increased purchases by roughly half, and the American administration has issued waivers allowing broader buying, undermining the very sanctions regime it had previously tightened.
sentences.translation: 인도는 구매량을 약 절반가량 늘렸고, 미국 행정부는 더 광범위한 구매를 허용하는 면제 조치를 발급하여 자신이 이전에 강화했던 바로 그 제재 체제를 약화시키고 있다.

grammarElements.text: has increased purchases by roughly half
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사구

grammarElements.text: has issued waivers allowing broader buying
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 + 현재분사 수식

grammarElements.text: undermining the very sanctions regime it had previously tightened
grammarElements.label: 결과 분사구문

vocabulary.example: The government issued temporary waivers to allow emergency imports.
Repeated policy reversals risk undermining public trust.

expressions.usage: 행위자 자신의 이전 행동과 현재 행동의 모순을 부각시키는 강조 표현

patterns.pattern: [Actor] has [action], undermining the very [system] it had previously [built/strengthened]
patterns.explanation: 어떤 행위자가 자신이 이전에 구축한 것을 스스로 약화시키는 아이러니를 표현하는 구조
examples: The central bank has cut rates, undermining the very stability it had previously fought to restore.
The regulator has relaxed rules, undermining the very framework it had previously strengthened.

drillQuestions.question: The American administration undermined the very sanctions regime it had previously ______.

sentences.notes: the very는 '바로 그'라는 강조를 통해 미국 정책의 자기 모순을 날카롭게 지적한다. it had previously tightened의 과거완료는 면제 발급보다 시간적으로 앞선 행동을 표시.

koreanAlignment.ko: 더 광범위한 구매를 허용하는 면제 조치를 발급했다

sentences.original: Yet high oil prices alone cannot solve Russia's deeper problems.
sentences.translation: 그러나 높은 유가만으로는 러시아의 더 깊은 문제들을 해결할 수 없다.

vocabulary.example: Money alone cannot guarantee success.

expressions.usage: 어떤 요소 하나만으로는 충분하지 않다는 한계를 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Yet [X] alone cannot [verb] [deeper/bigger problem]
patterns.explanation: 하나의 긍정적 요소만으로는 근본적 문제를 해결할 수 없음을 전환적으로 제시하는 구조
examples: Yet economic growth alone cannot solve the country's inequality crisis.
Yet technology alone cannot address the root causes of misinformation.

drillQuestions.question: High oil prices ______ cannot solve Russia's deeper problems.

sentences.notes: 문단 전환 문장으로, 짧고 단호하게 앞 문단의 '횡재' 논의를 뒤집는다. alone의 위치(명사 바로 뒤)가 핵심.

sentences.original: Ukrainian drone forces have achieved what analysts consider a turning point in the war: since December, verified Russian losses to drones have exceeded recruitment for the first time.
sentences.translation: 우크라이나 드론 부대는 분석가들이 전쟁의 전환점이라고 간주하는 것을 달성했다: 12월 이후 드론에 의한 확인된 러시아군 손실이 처음으로 신규 모집을 초과했다.

grammarElements.text: what analysts consider a turning point in the war
grammarElements.label: 관계절 목적어 (what절)

grammarElements.text: since December, verified Russian losses to drones have exceeded recruitment
grammarElements.label: 콜론 이후 부연 설명절

vocabulary.example: The discovery of antibiotics was a turning point in medical history.
Demand has exceeded supply for three consecutive quarters.

expressions.usage: 역사적으로 전례 없는 사건의 중요성을 부각시키는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] has achieved what [experts] consider [milestone]: [specific evidence]
patterns.explanation: 전문가 평가를 간접 인용하고 콜론 뒤에 구체적 근거를 제시하는 저널리즘 패턴
examples: The team has achieved what scientists consider a breakthrough: the vaccine showed ninety-five percent efficacy.
The startup has achieved what investors consider profitability: net income turned positive for two consecutive quarters.

drillQuestions.question: Verified Russian losses to drones have ______ recruitment for the first time.

sentences.notes: what analysts consider는 간접 인용의 세련된 표현. 콜론(:) 뒤에 구체적 데이터를 제시하는 저널리즘 기법에 주목.

koreanAlignment.en: what analysts consider a turning point

pronunciationNotes: exceeded [ɪkˈsiːdɪd] -- '익시이디드'로 두 번째 음절 강세와 장모음

sentences.original: A gamified targeting system that prioritizes individual infantry over armored vehicles has driven enemy casualties to nearly four hundred per day at peak periods, the equivalent of an entire battalion's assault component.
sentences.translation: 장갑차보다 개별 보병을 우선시하는 게임화된 표적 시스템이 적 사상자를 최고조 시기에 하루 거의 400명으로 끌어올렸으며, 이는 대대 전체 돌격 구성원에 해당하는 수치다.

grammarElements.text: A gamified targeting system that prioritizes individual infantry over armored vehicles
grammarElements.label: 관계절을 포함한 주어

grammarElements.text: enemy casualties to nearly four hundred per day
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + 결과 전치사구

grammarElements.text: the equivalent of an entire battalion's assault component
grammarElements.label: 동격 명사구

vocabulary.example: The gamified learning app motivated students to study more consistently.
Infantry units bore the heaviest casualties in the early weeks of the campaign.
A full battalion was deployed to secure the border region.

expressions.usage: 추상적 숫자를 독자가 실감할 수 있는 비교 대상으로 바꿔주는 표현

patterns.pattern: [System] has driven [metric] to [number], the equivalent of [vivid comparison]
patterns.explanation: 수치를 제시한 후 독자가 체감할 수 있는 비교 대상을 동격으로 덧붙이는 저널리즘 기법
examples: The heatwave has driven electricity consumption to record levels, the equivalent of powering an entire city.
The tax reform has driven capital outflows to fifty billion, the equivalent of the country's annual defense budget.

drillQuestions.question: The system has driven enemy casualties to nearly four hundred per day, the ______ of an entire battalion's assault component.

sentences.notes: gamified라는 현대적 용어와 battalion이라는 전통 군사 용어의 결합이 전쟁의 기술적 변화를 암시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: prioritizes individual infantry over armored vehicles

sentences.original: The exchange rate is starkly asymmetric, with four hundred Russian casualties for every Ukrainian one, at a material cost of under nine hundred dollars per kill.
sentences.translation: 교환 비율은 극명하게 비대칭적이어서, 우크라이나 측 사상자 1명당 러시아 측 400명이며, 물질적 비용은 건당 900달러 미만이다.

grammarElements.text: with four hundred Russian casualties for every Ukrainian one
grammarElements.label: with 부대상황 구문

grammarElements.text: at a material cost of under nine hundred dollars per kill
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (비용)

vocabulary.example: The conflict remained asymmetric, with one side vastly outgunning the other.
The data starkly illustrates the gap between rich and poor.

expressions.usage: 비율을 극적으로 표현할 때 사용하는 구조. one은 앞서 언급된 명사를 대체

patterns.pattern: [Ratio] is starkly [adjective], with [number A] for every [number B], at a cost of [amount]
patterns.explanation: 비율의 극단적 불균형을 수치와 비용으로 구체화하는 패턴
examples: The success rate is starkly uneven, with ten failures for every one success, at a cost of millions per attempt.
The trade balance is starkly imbalanced, with three imports for every one export, at a deficit of billions annually.

drillQuestions.question: The exchange rate is starkly ______, with four hundred Russian casualties for every Ukrainian one.

sentences.notes: exchange rate를 금융 용어가 아닌 전쟁 사상자 교환 비율에 적용한 것이 냉혹한 수사적 효과를 만든다. per kill이라는 표현의 건조함도 의도적.

koreanAlignment.en: The exchange rate is starkly asymmetric
under nine hundred dollars per kill

sentences.original: Russia continues to gain little ground despite these losses.
sentences.translation: 이러한 손실에도 불구하고 러시아는 별다른 영토를 확보하지 못하고 있다.

vocabulary.example: The opposition party has been gaining ground in recent polls.

expressions.usage: 큰 대가를 치르고도 성과가 없음을 강조하는 양보 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] continues to [verb] little [noun] despite [sacrifice]
patterns.explanation: 큰 희생에도 불구하고 미미한 성과를 계속 내고 있는 상황을 표현하는 구조
examples: The company continues to make little profit despite heavy investment.
The team continues to gain little traction despite months of effort.

drillQuestions.question: Russia continues to gain ______ ground despite these losses.

sentences.notes: 짧고 단호한 문장으로 앞선 통계의 결론을 내린다. little은 '거의 없는'이라는 부정적 의미로, few와의 차이(불가산/가산)에 주의.

sentences.original: The geopolitical dimension adds further complexity.
sentences.translation: 지정학적 차원이 상황에 더욱 복잡성을 더한다.

vocabulary.example: Geopolitical tensions have disrupted global supply chains.

expressions.usage: 이미 복잡한 상황에 새로운 복잡 요소가 추가됨을 나타내는 표현

patterns.pattern: The [adjective] dimension adds further [abstract noun]
patterns.explanation: 새로운 관점이나 차원이 기존 문제에 추가적 속성을 부여함을 나타내는 간결한 문단 도입 구조
examples: The cultural dimension adds further sensitivity to the debate.
The environmental dimension adds further urgency to the policy discussion.

drillQuestions.question: The geopolitical dimension adds further ______.

sentences.notes: 새로운 문단의 주제 전환 문장. 짧고 선언적인 구조로 독자의 주의를 새 주제로 이끈다.

sentences.original: Ukraine and its European allies are experiencing their first significant friction, driven by disputes over Russian oil still flowing through pipelines, disagreements over the pace of EU accession, and frustration at American disengagement.
sentences.translation: 우크라이나와 유럽 동맹국들은 처음으로 의미 있는 마찰을 겪고 있는데, 파이프라인을 통해 여전히 흐르고 있는 러시아 원유를 둘러싼 분쟁, EU 가입 속도에 대한 이견, 그리고 미국의 이탈에 대한 좌절감이 그 원인이다.

grammarElements.text: driven by disputes ..., disagreements ..., and frustration ...
grammarElements.label: 과거분사 수식 + 삼중 병렬

vocabulary.example: Turkey's accession to the EU has been under discussion for decades.
American disengagement from the region left a diplomatic vacuum.

expressions.usage: 현상의 원인을 분사 구문으로 제시할 때 사용하는 핵심 저널리즘 표현

patterns.pattern: [Actors] are experiencing [problem], driven by [cause 1], [cause 2], and [cause 3]
patterns.explanation: 문제를 제시한 뒤 세 가지 원인을 분사구문으로 병렬 열거하는 저널리즘 패턴
examples: Consumers are experiencing sticker shock, driven by rising wages, supply shortages, and higher tariffs.
Hospitals are experiencing staff shortages, driven by burnout, low pay, and emigration.

drillQuestions.question: The friction is driven by disputes, disagreements, and ______ at American disengagement.

sentences.notes: 삼중 병렬 구조(disputes, disagreements, frustration)가 마찰의 다면적 원인을 체계적으로 열거한다. 각 항목의 전치사(over, over, at)의 차이에 주목.

koreanAlignment.en: disputes over Russian oil still flowing through pipelines
koreanAlignment.ko: 파이프라인을 통해 흐르는 러시아 원유를 둘러싼 분쟁

sentences.original: Hungary has blocked a ninety-billion-euro support package over the pipeline dispute.
sentences.translation: 헝가리는 파이프라인 분쟁을 이유로 900억 유로 규모의 지원 패키지를 차단했다.

vocabulary.example: The opposition blocked the bill from passing through parliament.

expressions.usage: over가 '이유, 원인'을 나타내는 전치사로 사용된 경우. about보다 갈등적 뉘앙스가 강하다

patterns.pattern: [Country] has blocked [large-scale measure] over [specific dispute]
patterns.explanation: 한 국가가 특정 분쟁을 이유로 대규모 조치를 저지하는 구조
examples: Poland has blocked the trade agreement over agricultural subsidies.
Italy has blocked the migration pact over burden-sharing disputes.

drillQuestions.question: Hungary has blocked a ninety-billion-euro support package ______ the pipeline dispute.

sentences.notes: ninety-billion-euro가 하이픈으로 연결된 복합 형용사로 support package를 수식한다. 숫자를 문자로 쓰는 것이 저널리즘 문체의 특징.

sentences.original: Despite these tensions, the fundamental alignment between Ukraine and Europe remains intact, and both sides recognize they cannot afford a genuine rift.
sentences.translation: 이러한 긴장에도 불구하고 우크라이나와 유럽 간의 근본적 연대는 건재하며, 양측 모두 진정한 균열을 감당할 여유가 없음을 인식하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: the fundamental alignment between Ukraine and Europe
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: both sides recognize they cannot afford a genuine rift
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (that절 목적어)

vocabulary.example: Despite the scandal, the CEO's reputation remained largely intact.
The policy disagreement threatened to create a rift within the coalition.

expressions.usage: 재정적 의미뿐 아니라 전략적/정치적으로 감당하기 어려움을 나타낼 때도 사용

patterns.pattern: Despite [tensions], the fundamental [bond] remains intact, and both sides recognize [consequence]
patterns.explanation: 갈등에도 불구하고 핵심적 유대가 유지됨을 확인하고 양측의 인식을 덧붙이는 양보-확인 구조
examples: Despite the trade war, the fundamental partnership remains intact, and both sides recognize mutual dependence.
Despite internal divisions, the fundamental consensus remains intact, and all factions recognize the need for unity.

drillQuestions.question: The fundamental alignment remains ______, and both sides recognize they cannot afford a genuine rift.

sentences.notes: cannot afford는 금전적 의미가 아닌 '전략적으로 감당할 수 없다'는 뜻으로 사용된 점에 주목. genuine rift는 표면적 불화와 진짜 균열을 구분하는 표현.

sentences.original: Russia's windfall may buy time but not a path to victory.
sentences.translation: 러시아의 횡재는 시간을 벌어줄 수는 있지만 승리로 가는 길을 열어주지는 못한다.

grammarElements.text: but not a path to victory
grammarElements.label: 부정 대조 목적어

vocabulary.example: The inheritance was an unexpected windfall for the family.

expressions.usage: 근본적 해결이 아닌 일시적 유예를 얻을 때 사용하는 관용 표현

patterns.pattern: [X] may [verb] [A] but not [B]
patterns.explanation: 어떤 것이 제한적 효과만 있고 근본적 해결책은 되지 못함을 대조하는 간결한 구조
examples: Monetary easing may boost confidence but not structural growth.
The ceasefire may stop the shooting but not the underlying grievances.

drillQuestions.question: Russia's windfall may buy ______ but not a path to victory.

sentences.notes: 마지막 문단의 핵심 명제를 설정하는 압축적 도입문. A but not B 구조가 '횡재의 한계'를 한 문장으로 정의한다.

koreanAlignment.en: but not a path to victory

sentences.original: Every ten-dollar increase in oil prices delivers roughly 1.6 billion dollars to the Kremlin monthly, yet the military cannot convert money into territorial gains.
sentences.translation: 유가가 10달러 오를 때마다 매월 약 16억 달러가 크렘린에 전달되지만, 군은 돈을 영토적 성과로 전환할 수 없다.

grammarElements.text: Every ten-dollar increase in oil prices
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: yet the military cannot convert money into territorial gains
grammarElements.label: yet 대조절

vocabulary.example: The company struggled to convert its research breakthroughs into commercial products.
The agreement resolved the long-standing territorial dispute.

expressions.usage: 한 유형의 자원이나 성과를 다른 유형으로 바꾸려 할 때 사용하는 표현

patterns.pattern: Every [incremental change] delivers [benefit], yet [subject] cannot convert [resource] into [desired outcome]
patterns.explanation: 투입의 증가에도 불구하고 원하는 산출로 전환되지 않는 구조적 한계를 표현하는 패턴
examples: Every additional billion in spending delivers more capacity, yet the system cannot convert investment into improved services.
Every new hire delivers more manpower, yet the team cannot convert headcount into productivity.

drillQuestions.question: The military cannot ______ money into territorial gains.

sentences.notes: 돈은 들어오지만 전장 성과로 이어지지 않는 러시아의 딜레마를 수치와 대조로 압축한 문장. Every + 수치 표현은 경제 분석에서 자주 등장.

koreanAlignment.en: cannot convert money into territorial gains
koreanAlignment.ko: 돈을 영토적 성과로 전환할 수 없다

sentences.original: Oil production is declining at roughly three percent annually as investment is diverted to repairs, and the civilian economy has been hollowed out by mobilization and sanctions.
sentences.translation: 투자가 수리로 전용되면서 석유 생산량이 연간 약 3%씩 감소하고 있으며, 민간 경제는 동원과 제재로 속이 비어버렸다.

grammarElements.text: as investment is diverted to repairs
grammarElements.label: as 이유절

grammarElements.text: the civilian economy has been hollowed out by mobilization and sanctions
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (현재완료 수동)

vocabulary.example: Funds were diverted from education to defense spending.
Decades of underinvestment have hollowed out the country's infrastructure.

expressions.usage: 외양은 남아 있으나 실질적 내용이 사라진 상태를 비유적으로 표현

patterns.pattern: [Production] is declining at [rate] as [resource] is diverted to [other use], and [system] has been hollowed out by [cause]
patterns.explanation: 자원이 다른 곳으로 전용되면서 생산이 감소하고 시스템이 공동화되는 이중 악화를 표현하는 구조
examples: Manufacturing output is declining at five percent annually as labor is diverted to services, and the industrial base has been hollowed out by globalization.
Research funding is declining at two percent yearly as budgets are diverted to operations, and the innovation pipeline has been hollowed out by austerity.

drillQuestions.question: The civilian economy has been ______ out by mobilization and sanctions.

sentences.notes: hollowed out은 '속은 비었지만 껍데기는 남아 있다'는 강력한 은유. 러시아 경제의 구조적 취약성을 한 단어로 압축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: hollowed out by mobilization and sanctions

sentences.original: The sugar high from the Gulf crisis masks a slow deterioration that no amount of elevated crude prices can reverse.
sentences.translation: 걸프 위기에서 오는 일시적 흥분은 아무리 높은 원유 가격으로도 되돌릴 수 없는 느린 악화를 가리고 있다.

grammarElements.text: The sugar high from the Gulf crisis
grammarElements.label: 주어 (은유)

grammarElements.text: that no amount of elevated crude prices can reverse
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (부정 강조)

vocabulary.meaning: 설탕 섭취 후 일시적 흥분, 일시적 호황
vocabulary.example: The stimulus package produced a sugar high that faded within months.
The steady deterioration of public services has eroded trust in government.

expressions.usage: 어떤 수단으로도 해결이 불가능함을 강조하는 절대적 부정 표현

patterns.pattern: The [metaphorical boost] masks a [underlying problem] that no amount of [remedy] can [verb]
patterns.explanation: 표면적 호전이 근본적 문제를 감추고 있으며 어떤 수단으로도 해결 불가함을 표현하는 결론적 구조
examples: The tech boom masks a growing inequality that no amount of GDP growth can address.
The rally masks a structural weakness that no amount of government intervention can fix.

drillQuestions.question: The sugar high from the Gulf crisis ______ a slow deterioration.
No amount of elevated crude prices can ______ the deterioration.

sentences.notes: 기사의 마지막 문장. sugar high라는 의학/구어 은유가 러시아 경제의 일시적 회복을 '설탕 섭취 후 흥분'에 비유하며, 이것이 masks(가린다)하고 있는 것이 진짜 문제임을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: no amount of elevated crude prices can reverse
koreanAlignment.ko: 아무리 높은 원유 가격으로도 되돌릴 수 없는
